blocks.content: Maya arrived at two, which meant she'd been thinking about it all morning.

She brought coffee, which meant she was trying. She sat at the kitchen table with her hands wrapped around her cup and looked at Cassandra with the quiet, thorough attention of someone running a visual inventory.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: I didn't mean it as a compliment.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: Tell me you didn't practice this in the car.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: I practiced this in the car.

Maya set her cup down. She was twenty-four and had the particular stillness of someone who'd learned to moderate their reactions because they knew those reactions carried weight. She wasn't dramatic — she was worse than dramatic. She was thorough.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I already told you his name.
Cassandra looked at the coffee Maya had brought her — still too hot to drink.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: He's an artist. He's quiet, but not in a way that feels like absence. He notices things most people don't. He listens.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: It means I want to know how invested you are, because the more invested you are, the more—

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: You know what I'm going to say.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I know, and I want you to say it anyway. Out loud. So we can both hear how it sounds.
Maya looked at her — the expression Cassandra had known her whole life, the one that meant you are making this harder than it needs to be and I love you so much I could break, both at the same time.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: The more invested you are, the harder this is going to be. For you and for him.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: Yes. I know what I'm working with.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: Then you also know that you can't promise him things—

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: That you can't start something you don't know if you can finish—
Her voice stayed even, but something underneath it tensed.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: That's literally everyone, always, in every relationship anyone has ever had. Nobody knows if they can finish it.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: You know things other people don't know.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I know that time matters. Which is exactly why I'm not sitting at home watching it pass.
